title: UN Security Council Briefed: Gaza Ceasefire 'Increasingly Fragile' — 800+ Killed Since Oct 2025 Truce
detail: UN Assistant Secretary-General Khaled Khiari briefed the Security Council on April 28, 2026, warning that the October 10, 2025 Gaza ceasefire is 'increasingly fragile' as Israeli airstrikes, shelling, and armed activities by Hamas and other armed groups continue. Since the ceasefire began, approximately 800 Palestinians — including more than 200 children — and seven humanitarian workers have been killed by Israeli fire. Gaza's approximately 1.8 million displaced residents face water, sanitation, and health services described as 'again on the brink of collapse,' with 371,000+ housing units destroyed or damaged. The UN and EU jointly estimate $71.4 billion in recovery and reconstruction needs over the next decade, but less than $1 billion of $17 billion in pledged reconstruction funds has been received. Khiari noted that Phase II disarmament negotiations between Hamas and the Board of Peace have 'thus far not resulted in an agreement,' warning that failure to advance Phase Two significantly raises the risk of a return to full-scale hostilities. Security Council members called for maximum restraint from all parties and urged both sides to implement remaining Phase One obligations before Phase Two talks can progress.

media.caption: UN ASG Khiari briefs the Security Council on the Gaza ceasefire's fragility, April 28, 2026

title: Israeli Drone Strike Kills 9-Year-Old Adel Al-Najjar in Khan Younis; 4 More Killed in Gaza City — Day 201 Ceasefire
detail: An Israeli drone strike in eastern Khan Younis, southern Gaza, killed a nine-year-old boy, Adel Al-Najjar, on April 28, 2026 — the 201st day of the October 10, 2025 ceasefire. Palestinian medical sources reported his death at Nasser Hospital. The Israeli military stated it targeted an individual who posed a threat by approaching the 'yellow line' demarcating Israeli-controlled territory; the boy's family said he had gone to the area to collect cardboard for cooking fuel. In a separate incident the same day, an Israeli airstrike targeting a vehicle in Gaza City killed four more Palestinians and wounded several others. Gaza's Health Ministry confirmed that since the October 11, 2025 ceasefire began, at least 818 Palestinians have been killed and 2,301 wounded by Israeli fire — with the cumulative toll since October 7, 2023 exceeding 72,589+ killed and 172,375+ injured. Palestinian officials condemned the strikes as flagrant violations of the US-brokered ceasefire framework, while the UN Security Council the same day warned the truce is 'increasingly fragile.'

media.caption: 9-year-old Adel Al-Najjar killed by Israeli drone strike in eastern Khan Younis, April 28, 2026
